name: Competency Framework
description: Core, leadership, and functional competencies for Federal Civil Service officers

subcategories.name: Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
subcategories.description: Applied numerical reasoning and quantitative judgement.

questions.question: If a civil servant earns ₦150,000 monthly and receives a 15% allowance, what is the total monthly income
A Ministry budgeted ₦2,000,000 for a project but spent ₦1,850,000. What is the percentage savings
A Department has 120 staff. If 25% are on leave, how many are at work
An officer received ₦45,000 as housing allowance which is 20% of basic salary. What is the basic salary
The price of a file increased from ₦200 to ₦260. What is the percentage increase
questions.explanation: Increase = ₦60. % Increase = (60 ÷ 200) × 100 = 30%. Correction: Correct answer is 30%.

questions.question: A directorate has a budget of ₦5,000,000. If 40% is allocated to personnel costs, how much is left for other expenses
If an officer retires after 35 years of service, and pension is calculated as 80% of final salary ₦250,000, what is the monthly pension
What is ⅔ of 360
An officer is entitled to ₦120,000 annual leave grant. How much is this per month
If 60% of 500 staff are female, how many are male
questions.explanation: 60% of 500 = 300 females. Male staff = 500 - 300 = 200.

questions.question: A project was estimated at ₦4,500,000 but cost ₦4,725,000. What is the percentage cost overrun
If ⅗ of a Ministry’s budget goes to salaries, and the total budget is ₦15,000,000, how much goes to salaries
What is the simple interest on ₦100,000 at 10% per annum for 3 years
If the cost of 12 pens is ₦600, what is the cost of 30 pens
An officer receives ₦75,000 transport allowance, which is 25% of basic salary. What is the basic salary
If a file is processed in 6 hours by 3 officers, how many officers are needed to finish it in 2 hours
questions.explanation: Work is inversely proportional to time. Officers = 3 × (6 ÷ 2) = 9.

questions.question: What is the mean of the following salaries: ₦120,000, ₦130,000, ₦150,000, ₦100,000, ₦200,000
The ratio of male to female staff is 3:2. If there are 90 male staff, how many female staff are there
questions.explanation: 3 parts = 90 → 1 part = 30. Female = 2 parts = 60.

questions.question: An officer’s gross salary is ₦300,000. If deductions are 20%, what is the net salary
If the exchange rate is ₦750 to $1, how much is $200 in naira
If a civil servant earns ₦120,000 monthly, what is the annual salary
If monthly salary is ₦160,000, there are 20 working days and 8 working hours per day, what is the overtime hourly rate at 1.5×
A project cost fell from ₦500,000 to ₦425,000. What is the percentage decrease
A budget is split in the ratio 3:5 between Unit A and Unit B. If total = ₦800,000, how much does Unit B receive
An item costs ₦50,000. VAT at 7.5% is added. What is the total price including VAT
An allowance is 12% of a basic salary of ₦250,000. What is the allowance amount
Find the mean of ₦10,000, ₦20,000 and ₦30,000.
A machine cost ₦1,000,000 with salvage value ₦100,000 and useful life 5 years. Straight-line depreciation per year is:
Officer A completes a task in 6 hours, Officer B in 8 hours. Working together, how long will they take (hours and minutes)
What is 5% of ₦2,000,000
A salary of ₦200,000 is increased by 10% and then a 5% performance bonus is paid on the new salary. What is the bonus amount
A store had 500 units; 40% were used. How many units remain
A town of 1,000 people grows at 2% per annum. After 2 years the population is approximately:
What is the median of the set: 12, 15, 20, 22, 30
questions.explanation: Ordered list already; the middle (3rd) value is 20 → median = 20.

questions.question: A loan of ₦600,000 is to be repaid in 6 equal monthly installments. How much is each installment
questions.explanation: Installment = ₦600,000 ÷ 6 = ₦100,000 per month.

questions.question: An item priced at ₦10,000 is offered at a 12% discount. What is the discounted price
If an item costs ₦1,000 and inflation is 5% for one year, what is the new price
A budget of ₦1,200,000 is split 60:40 between Director A and Director B. How much does Director A receive
In a batch of 5 items 2 are defective. What is the probability (as a percentage) that a randomly selected item is defective
questions.explanation: Probability = 2 ÷ 5 = 0.4 = 40%.

questions.question: Convert 150 minutes into hours and minutes.
questions.explanation: 150 minutes ÷ 60 = 2 remainder 30 → 2 hours 30 minutes.

questions.question: If a civil servant is entitled to 30 working days annual leave, how many leave days accrue per month (assume equal monthly accrual)
questions.explanation: 30 days ÷ 12 months = 2.5 days per month.

questions.question: An officer earns ₦500,000. With 18% tax, what is the net pay
Three projects cost ₦240,000, ₦360,000 and ₦600,000. What fraction of the total cost is the smallest project
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (1/5.).

questions.question: Find the mode of the dataset: 12, 15, 15, 20, 22, 15, 30.
questions.explanation: 15 appears most frequently (three times) → mode = 15.

questions.question: A director’s performance score is weighted: 40% for targets (score 80) and 60% for behaviour (score 90). What is the weighted score
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (86.0.).

questions.question: Overtime is paid at 2× hourly rate. If monthly salary ₦120,000, 22 working days, 8 hours/day, what is 3 hours overtime payment
If managers:staff ratio is 1:12 and there are 6 managers, what is total staff including managers
questions.explanation: Staff per manager = 12. Staff = 6×12 = 72. Total including managers = 72 + 6 = 78.

questions.question: Compute compound growth: ₦100,000 at 5% per annum for 2 years (compound annually).
What is the range of the dataset: 45, 60, 75, 90, 100
questions.explanation: Range = max − min = 100 − 45 = 55. Correct option: A (55.).

questions.question: An item has two successive discounts 10% then 5% on ₦20,000. What is the final price
Convert the fraction 7/20 to a percentage.
questions.explanation: 7 ÷ 20 = 0.35 = 35%. (Correction: 7/20 = 0.35 = 35% — correct index should be 1.).

questions.question: Solve: x + 3x = 800, what is x
A budget of ₦1,000,000 is cut by 12.5% then increased by 20%. What is the final amount
If an allowance of ₦90,000 represents 30% of basic salary, what is the basic salary
Seven officers share ₦840,000 equally. How much does each receive
A vehicle consumes 9 litres per day. For 22 working days, how many litres are needed
questions.explanation: 9 × 22 = 198 litres.

questions.question: Exchange rate moves from ₦760/$ to ₦800/$. What is the percentage change (to 2 d.p.)
If training cost ₦450,000 and 15 participants share equally, how much per participant
questions.explanation: Per participant = 450,000 ÷ 15 = ₦30,000.

questions.question: A staff strength increases from 240 to 300. What is the percentage increase
A procurement lot of 120 units costs ₦72,000. What is unit cost
In the context of Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning, which action best demonstrates numerical & mathematical reasoning governance
options: Apply approved numerical & mathematical reasoning procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved numerical & mathematical reasoning procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: For effective numerical & mathematical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure numerical & mathematical reasoning compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on numerical & mathematical reasoning risk management within Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: When handling numerical & mathematical reasoning, which choice reflects proper numerical reasoning standards
options: Use quantitative evidence to support administrative decisions.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use quantitative evidence to support administrative decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain verbal reasoning in Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
options: Interpret official text accurately before action.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Interpret official text accurately before action. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: In the context of Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning, which action best demonstrates analytical judgement
options: Evaluate alternatives using logic and evidence.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Evaluate alternatives using logic and evidence. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: For effective numerical & mathematical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure problem solving
options: Break complex issues into testable and actionable parts.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Break complex issues into testable and actionable parts. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on communication clarity within Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
options: Present conclusions clearly with defensible rationale.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Present conclusions clearly with defensible rationale. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: When handling numerical & mathematical reasoning, which choice reflects proper documented procedure standards
options: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: In the context of Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning, which action best demonstrates public accountability
options: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: For effective numerical & mathematical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on risk control within Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
options: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: When handling numerical & mathematical reasoning, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain operational discipline in Numerical & Mathematical Reasoning
options: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in numerical & mathematical reasoning.

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: What percentage of the revenue allocation formula currently in operation goes to the Federal Government?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (52.68%). The Revenue Allocation formula in operation among the three tiers of government is: Federal Government 52.68%

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: The Public Procurement Act 2007 applies to entities that derive at least what percentage of funds for any procurement from the Federation Share of Consolidated Revenue Fund?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (35%). The Act applies to entities that derive at least 35% of funds appropriated for any procurement from the Federation Share of Consolidated Revenue Fund.

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes the workflow for an officer to access their National Housing Fund (NHF) contributions?
options: The officer must exit the service and can access their contributions within one month of retirement. a
The officer must apply directly to the National Housing Fund.
The officer must wait for a circular from the Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
The officer must provide a stamped death certificate.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The officer must exit the service and can access their contributions within one month of retirement. a). Rule 021004 states that officers who exit the Service "must access their National Housing Funds (NHF) contributions within one month of retirement."

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: Which body is accountable for monitoring the accruals to and disbursement of revenue from the Federation Account?
options: The Revenue Mobilization Allocation and Fiscal Commission
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Revenue Mobilization Allocation and Fiscal Commission). The Revenue Mobilization Allocation and Fiscal Commission is one of the Federal Executive Bodies... Among other functions, the Commission has the power to: monitor the accruals to and disbursement of revenue from the Federation Account

questions.question: which role projects estimates of oil and non-oil revenues in consultation with revenue generating agencies?
options: The Budget Office of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Budget Office of the Federation). The Budget Office of the Federation, in consultation with the revenue generating agencies of the Federal Government, projects estimates of oil and non-oil revenues

questions.question: In official practice, choose the option that a role of the Permanent Secretary, in relation to other government bodies?
options: A memorandum to the Executive Council of the Federation for funds may have to be cleared with the Ministry of Finance.
Directly controlling the National Planning Commission.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (A memorandum to the Executive Council of the Federation for funds may have to be cleared with the Ministry of Finance.). The Permanent Secretary in a Ministry may need to clear a memorandum for 406 funds with the Ministry of Finance before it is sent to the Executive Council of the Federation.

questions.question: In the public service context, responsibility for is the Chief Adviser to the Minister in a Ministry belongs to which role?
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Permanent Secretary.). The Minister's Chief Adviser is the Permanent Secretary.

questions.question: In official practice, select the statement that correctly defines the role of the National Council on Establishments?
options: To set the salary for all government workers.
To manage the budget of the Federal Government.
To hire and fire civil servants. □.
To facilitate inter-governmental consultations on establishment matters and to avoid unnecessary disparities in general conditions of service in all the Civil Services of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To facilitate inter-governmental consultations on establishment matters and to avoid unnecessary disparities in general conditions of service in all the Civil Services of the Federation.). The National Council on Establishments was established to facilitate inter-governmental consultations on establishment matters and to avoid unnecessary disparities in general conditions of service in all the Civil Services of the Federation.

questions.question: In official practice, which role is encouraged to study and imbibe these rules and their associated and complementary instructions, guidelines, and extant circulars?
options: Only the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
Only new entrants into the Service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (All Public Servants.). The Preamble states, "Public Servants are, therefore, implored to study and imbibe these Rules and their associated and complementary Instructions, Guidelines, and extant Circulars..."

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: What does "Permanent Secretary" mean?
options: The Chief Executive Officer of a Parastatal.
Officer in charge of day-to-day administration in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Offices.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation. Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Officer in charge of day-to-day administration in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Offices.). Rule 010106 defines "Permanent Secretary" as "Officer in charge of day-to-day administration in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Offices."

questions.question: Within government administration, which role are the members of the Junior Staff Committee (JSC) and Senior Staff Committee (SSC) in Parastatals? 20?
options: The representative of the Department of Human Resource Management and an observer from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation and the Permanent Secretary
The Board of Directors of the Parastatala
The Chief Executive Officer and the Financial Officer
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The representative of the Department of Human Resource Management and an observer from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation). Rule 020305 states that the membership of the JSC and SSC in Parastatals includes a representative of the Department of Human Resource Management and an observer from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

questions.question: In official practice, which office is accountable for paying the salary of an officer on secondment?
options: The parent MDA that released the officer.
The Office of the Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
The MDA where the officer is seconded to.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The MDA where the officer is seconded to.). Rule 020504 states that the MDA with the higher salary must be accountable for the payment of the salary and non-salary allowances should be paid by the receiving MDA49.

questions.question: which office is accountable for posting and redeploying all senior staff?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office a
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office a). The rules state that all senior staff can be posted and/or redeployed to new posts in accordance with the approved Employee Mobility Policy52.

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes the overall goal of the pension scheme outlined in Chapter 19?
options: To provide short-term loans to employees. □
To secure proper administration and accounting of pension contributions.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To secure proper administration and accounting of pension contributions.). The chapter details the administration, responsibilities of various entities, and accounting procedures for the pension scheme, indicating its goal is proper management of contributions.

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: According to Section 2 of the Finance (Control and Management) Act of 1958, what does "Public Finance" include?
options: The Public Revenue of the Federation and any moneys held in an official capacity by a Public Service Officer or Government agent
Any moneys held by a private citizen
Only the Public Revenue of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Public Revenue of the Federation and any moneys held in an official capacity by a Public Service Officer or Government agent). Public Finance includes "The Public Revenue of the Federation" and "Any monies held in his official capacity... by any Officer in the Public Service of the Federation or by any agent of the government".

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: Which of these is a source of revenue for the Consolidated Revenue Fund?
options: Proceeds from domestic consumption of crude oil
Proceeds of tax under disbursement-as-you-earn system from police personnel
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Share from the Federation Account). Sources of revenue for the Consolidated Revenue Fund include "Share from the Federation Account”.

questions.question: choose the best answer for: The Development Fund is primarily meant for what type of projects?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Capital projects). This Fund is meant for capital projects. It is established by the 1999 Constitution.

questions.question: In official practice, choose the best answer for: What does the President's directives to the Minister of Finance and the Budget Office of the Federation aim to achieve through the budget?
options: To reduce poverty, generate wealth and create jobs
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To reduce poverty, generate wealth and create jobs). The plans indicate what the Government intends to do to reduce poverty, generate wealth and create jobs

questions.question: Within government administration, which role is the Accounting Officer for a Ministry?
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Permanent Secretary). The Permanent Secretary of a Ministry is designated as the Accounting Officer and is in charge for all expenditures and revenue.

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: The 1999 Constitution defines the "Civil Service of the Federation" as service in a civil capacity as staff of which of the following?
options: A Ministry or department of the Government of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A Ministry or department of the Government of the Federation.). The 1999 Constitution defines the Civil Service of the Federation as service in a civil capacity as staff of the Office of the President, the Vice-President, a Ministry, or a department of the government.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: The political head of a Ministry is the Minister, but who is the administrative head?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Permanent Secretary). The Minister is the political head of the Ministry, and the Permanent Secretary serves as the administrative head.

questions.question: In the public service context, which office is the Accounting Officer of a Ministry?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation □.
The Director of Finance and Accounts
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Permanent Secretary). The Permanent Secretary is the Ministry's Accounting Officer.

questions.question: which option best describes the chief role of the Auditor-General for the Federation?
options: To control the release of funds to MDAs. □.
To manage all government bank accounts.
To check and report on government expenditures and public accounts.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To check and report on government expenditures and public accounts.). The Auditor-General is the Head of the Supreme Audit Institution of the country and is in charge for auditing and reporting on the public accounts of the Federation.

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: The handbook advises against using official working time for which of the following activities?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Personal errands and private business.). The Civil Servant is expected to give his best in the execution of his official duties, and should not use official working time for personal errands or private business

questions.question: In official practice, choose the best answer for: A public officer must submit a declaration of assets and liabilities at what frequency?
options: At the beginning and end of their term of office and at the end of every four years.
Only at the beginning of their term.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (At the beginning and end of their term of office and at the end of every four years.). Every public officer must, within 3 months after the coming into force of this Code of Conduct or immediately after taking office and thereafter at the end of every four years and at the end of his term of office, submit a written declaration of all his properties, assets, and liabilities to the Code of Conduct Bureau.

subcategories.name: Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
subcategories.description: Verbal analysis, logical comprehension, and critical reasoning.

questions.question: What are the three main categories of competencies in the Federal Civil Service
questions.explanation: The competency framework includes Core, Leadership, and Functional competencies.

questions.question: Choose the word that is most similar in meaning to 'prerogative
questions.explanation: 'Prerogative' refers to a special right or privilege exclusive to a person or group. The closest synonym here is 'Right'.

questions.question: Which of the following is the opposite of 'transparent'
questions.explanation: The opposite of transparent (clear, see-through) is opaque (not allowing light to pass through).

questions.question: Which word correctly completes the sentence: 'The officer was praised for his ______ in handling the matter'
questions.explanation: The correct word is 'tact' meaning diplomacy or sensitivity in dealing with issues.

questions.question: Select the pair with the same relationship: 'Doctor: Hospital':: 'Teacher: '
questions.explanation: Just as a doctor works in a hospital, a teacher works in a school.

questions.question: Which word is most opposite in meaning to 'benevolent'
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'benevolent' (kind, generous) is 'malevolent' (evil, harmful).

questions.question: Identify the correctly spelt word
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'Accommodate' with double 'c' and double 'm'.

questions.question: What is the plural of 'crisis'
questions.explanation: The plural of 'crisis' is 'crises'.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Dog, Cat, Cow, Chair.
questions.explanation: Dog, Cat, and Cow are animals, but Chair is an object.

questions.question: Which word is most similar in meaning to 'expedite'
questions.explanation: 'Expedite' means to hasten or speed up a process.

questions.question: Choose the correct preposition: 'He insisted ___ his right to appeal.
questions.explanation: The correct phrase is 'insisted on'.

questions.question: Select the correctly punctuated sentence
options: The Permanent Secretary said 'I will attend tomorrow's meeting.'.
The Permanent Secretary said, 'I will attend the meeting.'.
The Permanent Secretary said, I will attend the meeting promptly.'.
The Permanent Secretary, said I will attend tomorrow's briefing.
questions.explanation: The correct punctuation is: The Permanent Secretary said, 'I will attend the meeting.'.

questions.question: Which of the following is the antonym of 'diligent'
questions.explanation: The antonym of 'diligent' is 'careless'.

questions.question: Which word best completes the sentence: 'The committee reached a unanimous ______ on the issue.
questions.explanation: The correct completion is 'decision'.

questions.question: Select the word that is opposite in meaning to 'scarce
questions.explanation: The opposite of scarce is plentiful.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Pen, Pencil, Paper, Book.
questions.explanation: Pen, Pencil, and Book are durable items, while Paper is consumable.

questions.question: Choose the synonym for 'meticulous
questions.explanation: 'Meticulous' means showing great attention to detail → synonym is 'Thorough'.

questions.question: What is the correct indirect speech form of: He said, 'I am ready'
options: He said that he is ready.
He said he will be ready.
He said he had been ready.
questions.explanation: In indirect speech, present tense changes to past: 'He said he was ready.'.

questions.question: Which pair is correctly matched as synonyms: 'Expand: '
questions.explanation: 'Expand' means to increase.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: 'She is very good ___ mathematics.
questions.explanation: The correct phrase is 'good at' something.

questions.question: Select the correctly spelt word
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'Conscientious'.

questions.question: Choose the word that is most opposite in meaning to 'expand
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'expand' is 'contract'.

questions.question: What is the synonym of 'ambiguous'
questions.explanation: 'Ambiguous' means unclear or uncertain.

questions.question: Which of the following completes the proverb: 'A stitch in time saves ____'
questions.explanation: The correct proverb is 'A stitch in time saves nine'.

questions.question: Which of these is the antonym of 'rigid'
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'rigid' (stiff, inflexible) is 'flexible'.

questions.question: Which of these words is synonymous with 'impartial'
questions.explanation: The synonym of 'impartial' is 'neutral' (fair and unbiased).

questions.question: Which word best completes the sentence: 'The officer was known for his strict ______ to rules.
questions.explanation: The correct word is 'adherence'.

questions.question: Select the odd one out: Apple, Mango, Banana, Chair
questions.explanation: Apple, Mango, and Banana are fruits; Chair is not.

questions.question: Which of the following is a synonym for 'obsolete'
questions.explanation: 'Obsolete' means outdated or no longer in use.

questions.question: Which word correctly completes the sentence: 'The manager gave a detailed ______ of the incident.
questions.explanation: The correct word is 'account'.

questions.question: Find the word most opposite in meaning to 'optimistic':
questions.explanation: The opposite of optimistic (hopeful) is pessimistic (expecting the worst).

questions.question: What is the synonym of 'vivid'
questions.explanation: 'Vivid' means clear, bright, or detailed.

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect speech: She said, 'I will help you.
options: She said that she will help me.
She said that she would help me.
questions.explanation: In indirect speech, 'will' changes to 'would': She said that she would help me.

questions.question: Which of these is the antonym of 'generous'
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'generous' is 'selfish'.

questions.question: Which of the following words is closest in meaning to 'lucid'
questions.explanation: 'Lucid' means clear or easy to understand.

questions.question: Choose the correct completion: 'The committee was divided ______ the proposal.
questions.explanation: The correct phrase is 'divided on' an issue.

questions.question: Which proverb is correctly completed: 'Too many cooks spoil the ____'
questions.explanation: The correct form is 'Too many cooks spoil the broth.'.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a synonym for 'happy'
questions.explanation: Miserable is the opposite of happy, not a synonym.

questions.question: Choose the word that best completes: 'The new system will help to ______ efficiency.
questions.explanation: The correct word is 'improve efficiency'.

questions.question: Select the word closest in meaning to 'resilient
questions.explanation: 'Resilient' means able to recover quickly; closest synonym is 'Strong'.

questions.question: Which of these words is the antonym of 'complex'
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'complex' is 'simple'.

questions.question: Which of the following is a synonym for 'commence'
questions.explanation: 'Commence' means to begin.

questions.question: Choose the correctly spelt word
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'Definitely'.

questions.question: Which of these words is closest in meaning to 'benevolent'
questions.explanation: 'Benevolent' means kind and charitable.

questions.question: What is the antonym of 'artificial'
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'artificial' is 'natural'.

questions.question: Choose the word that best completes: 'The new rules will ______ corruption in the service.
questions.explanation: The correct word is 'curb' (to control or limit).

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Book, Pen, Teacher, Pencil.
questions.explanation: Book, Pen, and Pencil are objects; Teacher is a person.

questions.question: Which of the following is closest in meaning to 'candid'
questions.explanation: 'Candid' means open and honest.

questions.question: Which of these is the antonym of 'expand'
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'expand' is 'shrink'.

questions.question: Which word correctly completes: 'The officer was praised for his ______ in handling the crisis.
questions.explanation: The correct word is 'tact', meaning diplomacy and sensitivity.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'brief
questions.explanation: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.question: Which proverb is correctly completed: 'The pen is mightier than the ____'
questions.explanation: The correct proverb is 'The pen is mightier than the sword.'.

questions.question: What is the antonym of 'prosperity'
questions.explanation: The opposite of prosperity (wealth and success) is poverty.

questions.question: Which of these words is synonymous with 'fragile'
questions.explanation: 'Fragile' means delicate or easily broken.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: 'She is interested ___ music.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition is 'interested in'.

questions.question: Select the correct indirect speech: He said, 'I am going to Abuja tomorrow.
options: He said he is going to Abuja tomorrow.
He said he was going to Abuja the next day.
He said he goes to Abuja tomorrow.
He said he will go to Abuja the next day.
questions.explanation: In indirect speech, 'am going' becomes 'was going' and 'tomorrow' changes to 'the next day'.

questions.question: What is the antonym of 'rapid'
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'rapid' (fast) is 'slow'.

questions.question: Which of these words is closest in meaning to 'precise'
questions.explanation: 'Precise' means exact or accurate.

questions.question: Which word best completes: 'The Head of Service issued a strong ______ on discipline.
questions.explanation: The correct word is 'directive'.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Chair, Table, Bench, Bread.
questions.explanation: Chair, Table, and Bench are furniture; Bread is food.

questions.question: In the context of Verbal & Analytical Reasoning, which action best demonstrates verbal & analytical reasoning governance
options: Apply approved verbal & analytical reasoning procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved verbal & analytical reasoning procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: For effective verbal & analytical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure verbal & analytical reasoning compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on verbal & analytical reasoning risk management within Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: When handling verbal & analytical reasoning, which choice reflects proper numerical reasoning standards
options: Use quantitative evidence to support administrative decisions.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use quantitative evidence to support administrative decisions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain verbal reasoning in Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
options: Interpret official text accurately before action.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Interpret official text accurately before action. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: In the context of Verbal & Analytical Reasoning, which action best demonstrates analytical judgement
options: Evaluate alternatives using logic and evidence.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Evaluate alternatives using logic and evidence. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: For effective verbal & analytical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure problem solving
options: Break complex issues into testable and actionable parts.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Break complex issues into testable and actionable parts. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on communication clarity within Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
options: Present conclusions clearly with defensible rationale.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Present conclusions clearly with defensible rationale. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: When handling verbal & analytical reasoning, which choice reflects proper documented procedure standards
options: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: In the context of Verbal & Analytical Reasoning, which action best demonstrates public accountability
options: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: For effective verbal & analytical reasoning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on risk control within Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
options: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: When handling verbal & analytical reasoning, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain operational discipline in Verbal & Analytical Reasoning
options: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in verbal & analytical reasoning.

questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of the legal framework demand for processing short-term engagements?
options: A circular from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The negotiation, drafting, and execution of contracts of engagement by the parties.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The negotiation, drafting, and execution of contracts of engagement by the parties.). Rule 020421 states that due processes are followed in the "negotiation, drafting, and execution of contracts of engagement by the parties on all the short-term appointments." Part 7: Transfers and Secondments

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: Any directive to the Accounting Officer by a Political Head having financial implications must be:?
options: Only for minor financial matters. Q
Approved by the National Assembly first.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (In writing. 153). Financial Regulation 117(i) states, "Any directive to the Accounting Officer by a Political Head having financial implications on the ministry, agency or parastatal must be in writing."

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: What must a Revenue Collector do immediately upon receiving money?
options: Keep the money in a safe until the end of the week □ 158
Enter the receipt in their cash book without delay
Issue a verbal receipt and collect a written one later
Hand it over to the Sub-Accounting Officer
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Enter the receipt in their cash book without delay). Financial Regulation 209(i) necessitate every Revenue Collector to keep a cash book and enter all receipts without delay on the day of receipt.

questions.question: According to established rules, which option aligns with the meaning of the demand procedure for a lodgement made by an officer who is neither a Revenue Collector nor a Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: It must be accompanied by a hand-written note
It must be supported by a paying-in form (Treasury Form 15)
It must be entered in a private cash booka 161
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (It must be supported by a paying-in form (Treasury Form 15)). Financial Regulation 218 specifies that the lodgement will be supported by a paying-in form (Treasury Form 15).

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: What must be kept in each strong-room or reserve cash safe for all cash, stamps, receipt/licence books, and other articles deposited?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Registers.). Financial Regulation 1121 demand that "Registers must be kept in each strong-room or reserve cash safe of all cash, stamps, receipt or licence books and other articles deposited."

questions.question: select the option that best answers: Question: What must be obtained when receipt and licence books are handed over from one officer to another?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (A handing-over certificate.). Financial Regulation 1226 states, "When receipt and licence books are handed over from one officer to another, a handing-over certificate must be obtained."

questions.question: which role is in charge for keeping the up-to-date personal log of every officer?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The respective Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office). Rule 020107 mandates that Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices must keep an up-to-date log of every officer under their control.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the definition of "volunteerism" according to the rules?
options: Offering services for a short-term contract. 24 Q
Offering one’s time or talent for charitable, educational, or other worthwhile activities, especially in one’s community, without remuneration
Offering one's time or talent for a fee.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Offering one’s time or talent for charitable, educational, or other worthwhile activities, especially in one’s community, without remuneration). Rule 020416 defines volunteerism as a "practice of offering one’s time or talent for charitable, educational, or other worthwhile activities, especially in one’s community."

questions.question: Within government administration, which office is ultimately accountable for ensuring that the approved fees are paid to the deserving invigilators and examiners?
options: The Director of Human Resources Q
The Permanent Secretary of the MDA
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Permanent Secretary of the MDA). The Permanent Secretary is accountable for the overall supervision of the examinations, including the disbursement of fees 59 Section B: COMPUTER-BASED TEST (CBT) PROMOTION EXAMINATION FOR OFFICERS IN GRADES 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION ON PUBLIC SERVICE RULE (PSR). Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to secure you attempt every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options. Part 1: Employment Consideration for Applicants Above 50 Years in the Public Service

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: If a disbursement is made on account for a contract, what is the money claimed limited to?
options: The amount available in the vote
An arbitrary percentage of the contract.
The cost of the work certified to have been performed.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The cost of the work certified to have been performed.). Financial Regulation 412 states: "In the case o. payment on account, no money will be claimed other than the cost of the work certified to have been performed."

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: What must be done with the cancelled receipt or licence, including the original?
options: It will be retained in the receipt or licence book.
It should be given to the payer. □.
It should be sent to the Accountant-General.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (It will be retained in the receipt or licence book.). Financial Regulation 1216 specifies that the "cancelled receipts or licence, including the original, will be retained in the receipt or licence book."

questions.question: In the public service context, which option best describes necessitate to support a withdrawal from a deposit?
options: The original receipt issued when the money was placed on deposit
A handwritten note from the depositor
A verbal confirmation from the Accounting Officera
A photocopy of the original receipt
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The original receipt issued when the money was placed on deposit). Financial Regulation 1306 states that withdrawals from deposit must be supported by the original receipt issued when the money was placed on deposit.

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: What type of report must the Board of Survey prepare and submit?
options: A report in triplicate, which must be signed by all members.
A verbal report to the Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (A report in triplicate, which must be signed by all members.). Financial Regulation 1807 states that the Board of Survey must prepare a report in triplicate, signed by all members. 279

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the chief method of tracking the usage of government vehicles?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Logbooks.). Financial Regulation 2005-2007 consistently emphasizes the use and maintenance of the logbook for tracking vehicle usage.

questions.question: In official practice, which office is the Chief Adviser to the Minister and also the Ministry's Accounting Officer?
options: The Chairman of the Extra-Ministerial Department
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federationa 374
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Permanent Secretary). His Chief Adviser is the Permanent Secretary who is also the Ministry's Accounting Officer.

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the ultimate aim of the Due workflow policy's objective to guarantee the elimination of waste?
options: To prolong the duration of projects
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To secure value for money). The objective to eliminate waste is directly linked to the aim of achieving value for money.

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: In the context of a Public Accounts Committee hearing, what is the role of the Accountant-General of the Federation?
options: To represent all MDAs and answer for them
To provide clarification on accounting procedures and financial regulations
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To provide clarification on accounting procedures and financial regulations). The Accountant-General of the Federation attends the committee's sittings and provides clarification on accounting procedures, treasury circulars, and financial regulations

questions.question: at what point should File Notes be made frequently?
options: When verbal discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.
Never, as they are not formal. □. 490
Only at the end of the day.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (When verbal discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.). They should, therefore, be made as frequently as possible when verbal discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.

questions.question: Within government administration, at what point should File Notes be made frequently?
options: Only at the end of the day.
When verbal discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.
Never, as they are not formal.3. 498
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (When verbal discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.). They should, therefore, be made as frequently as possible when verbal discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: According to Financial Regulation 202, what must an officer receiving public money issue for every sum of money paid to the government?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (An official receipt). Financial Regulation 202 mandates that an official receipt must be issued by the officer receiving public money for every sum paid to the government.

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: How is a cheque or bank draft returned to a sender, redirected, or remitted to another station recorded?
options: An appropriate entry is made in the Paper Money Register.
By a verbal notification to the sender.
It is recorded in the General Ledger.
The cheque is simply returned without any log.a 163
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (An appropriate entry is made in the Paper Money Register.). Financial Regulation 233(iii) specifies that an appropriate entry will be made in the Paper Money Register, giving a reference to the covering memorandum or remittance voucher.

questions.question: which role can effect an alteration to the classification of a voucher?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The officer controlling expenditure.). Financial Regulation 421 states: "An alteration to the classification of a voucher must only be effected by the officer controlling expenditure who will amend his Vote Book accordingly."

questions.question: Within government administration, responsibility for is accountable for providing adequate accounting systems and controls in ministries and extra-ministerial offices belongs to which role?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts. □ 199
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Accountant-General of the Federation.). Financial Regulation 106 states: "The Accountant-General of the Federation is the Head of the Federal Government Accounting Services and the Treasury. He has the duty for providing adequate accounting systems and controls in the ministries, extra-ministerial offices and other arms of Government."

questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of the procedure if an alteration to the amount of a voucher is necessary?
options: A new voucher must be prepared.
The alteration can be made and stamped "Corrected".
The alteration can be initiated by the certifying officer.
The Head of Finance and Accounts can approve the alteration
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (A new voucher must be prepared.). Financial Regulation 610 states: "Alterations to the amount of a voucher whether in words or figure are not allowed. A new voucher must be prepared when necessary." 204

questions.question: In the public service context, which option correctly addresses: What must be stamped on every cheque leaf?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The correct account number.). Financial Regulation 703(i) states: "Every cheque leaf must be crossed and stamped with the correct account number."
